text.title: database-selection Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for database-selection. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: Relational (PostgreSQL, MySQL) for ACID transactions and relational integrity. NewSQL (CockroachDB, TiDB, Spanner, YugabyteDB) for horizontally scaled SQL with strong consistency. Document (MongoDB, DynamoDB, Couchbase) for nested or evolving schema. Key-Value (Redis, Memcached, Valkey) for cache, sessions, leaderboards. Time-Series (TimescaleDB, InfluxDB, ClickHouse, QuestDB) for metrics, telemetry, append-heavy workloads. Graph (Neo4j, Neptune, ArangoDB) for many-hop relationship queries. Vector (Qdrant, Weaviate, Pinecone, pgvector) for embedding similarity search. Search (Elasticsearch, OpenSearch, Meilisearch, Typesense) for full-text and faceted retrieval.
rule.rationale: Each paradigm is optimized for one access pattern. Forcing a paradigm onto the wrong workload causes 10x cost or 10x latency.

rule.statement: CP (Consistency + Partition Tolerance) — finance, ledgers, inventory. AP (Availability + Partition Tolerance) — social feeds, streaming, telemetry. CA (Consistency + Availability) — single-region OLTP where partitions are rare. PACELC: when no Partition, choose PC (latency cost for consistency, e.g. Spanner) or PA (lower latency, eventual consistency, e.g. Cassandra/DynamoDB default).
rule.rationale: CAP forces a choice during a network partition; PACELC forces a choice during normal operation. Both shape the user-visible failure mode.
